notes: Skill shows consistent advantage across all 3 evals: +44% average pass rate improvement
Eval 1 (atomize-transformer) shows the largest gap: 100% with skill vs 44% without — skill enforces directory structure, naming conventions, and log format
Eval 2 (query-comparison): with_skill cites atoms (4 citations) and includes confidence statement; without_skill ignores the knowledge base entirely and writes from general knowledge
Eval 3 (atomize-efficient): with_skill properly handles contradictions (2 CONTRADICTION blocks with Resolution); without_skill finds contradictions but fails to update existing atom source fields
Common failure mode with_skill: assertion 5 (file new atoms) fails when relevant atom already exists — the agent doesn't detect it needs to update rather than create
Common failure mode with_skill: index.md occasionally misses newly created atoms (eval 3 — comparison atom missing from index)
Without-skill consistently fails on: source field naming (source vs sources), directory conventions (sources/ vs raw/), log format specification, and not using the knowledge base at all for queries
The +35s time overhead and +12K token cost for the skill is justified by the significant quality improvement (44% higher pass rate)
